The factorial of a number is one of the most common mathematical operations used in programming. It is widely applied in combinatorics, probability, permutations, combinations, recursive algorithms & mathematical computations. Learning how to calculate the factorial of a number is an excellent way to understand loops, recursion & functions in JavaScript.
What is the Factorial of a Number?
The factorial of a non-negative integer n is the product of all positive integers from 1 to n.
Mathematical Formula
n! = n × (n − 1) × (n − 2) × … × 2 × 1
Special case:
0! = 1
Example :
Input:
5
Calculation:
5!
= 5 × 4 × 3 × 2 × 1
= 20 × 3 × 2 × 1
= 60 × 2 × 1
= 120 × 1
= 120
Output:
120
Algorithm

• Read the number.

• Initialize a variable factorial with 1.

• Multiply the variable by every integer from 1 to n.

• Store the result.

• Print the factorial.
Method 1: Using a for Loop
The for loop is the simplest & most commonly used method to calculate a factorial. It iterates from 1 to the given number & continuously multiplies the running result by the current value.
JavaScript Program
let number = 5;
let factorial = 1;
for (let i = 1; i <= number; i++) {
factorial *= i;
}
console.log("Factorial =", factorial);Output :
Factorial = 120
Time Complexity
O(n)
Space Complexity
O(1)
Method 2: Using a while Loop
The while loop performs the same calculation but continues executing until the specified condition becomes false. It is useful when the number of iterations is determined dynamically during execution. The logic remains simple while demonstrating another looping construct.
JavaScript Program
let number = 5;
let factorial = 1;
let i = 1;
while (i <= number) {
factorial *= i;
i++;
}
console.log("Factorial =", factorial);Output
Factorial = 120
Time Complexity
O(n)
Space Complexity
O(1)
Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)
1. What is the factorial of 0?
The factorial of 0 is defined as 1.
2. Which method is best for calculating factorial in JavaScript?
The for loop is the most efficient & beginner-friendly approach because it uses constant extra space & is easy to understand.
Conclusion
The Calculating the factorial of a number is a classic programming problem that helps developers understand loops, recursion, functions & algorithm design in JavaScript. While the traditional for loop remains the most efficient & widely recommended approach methods such as recursion, while loops, reduce() & reusable functions demonstrate different programming styles & concepts.
